document.title: Butterfly Guy Application Architecture
document.purpose: Machine-oriented architecture handoff for the next coding agent.

freshness.warning: Compare graph_commit with git rev-parse HEAD before trusting graph-derived edges.

system.name: Butterfly Guy
system.type: Automated 0-DTE butterfly options trading and research platform
system.critical_convention: Paper fills use mark price (bid + ask) / 2; live mode requires explicit safety guards.

external_systems.name: Grafana
roles: trading, trade detail, and performance dashboards

external_systems.name: Alertmanager
roles: critical live reconciliation and settlement alerts

external_systems.name: Discord
roles: SPX trade events, charts, scans, reports

startup_sequence: Load and validate YAML plus environment configuration
Initialize database pool and run ordered SQL migrations
Initialize Schwab client and query repositories
Classify market regime from daily bars and VIX
Construct risk, order, trade, position, and collector services
Reconcile broker and DB state in live mode
Recover open trade and persisted peak, then reseed metrics/risk state

concurrent_loops.name: collector
concurrent_loops.responsibility: Fetch chain/spot/daily-bar snapshots and persist them.

concurrent_loops.name: entry_loop
concurrent_loops.responsibility: Attempt one eligible entry, then monitor the open trade.

concurrent_loops.name: daily_reset
concurrent_loops.responsibility: Initialize/reset per-session risk and metrics state.

concurrent_loops.name: broker_reconciler
concurrent_loops.responsibility: Compare broker positions/orders to DB trades/intents and gate entries on ambiguity.

concurrent_loops.name: eod_charts
concurrent_loops.responsibility: Generate and send end-of-day trade charts.

invariants: DB and broker option quantities must match
ambiguous fills do not silently become trades
expired cash-settled positions need transaction evidence

market_data: SchwabClientWrapper fetches option chains and spots
Live entry, monitoring parity, dashboards, and DB backtests consume the history
entry: RiskEngine checks daily/weekly loss, trade count, consecutive losses, position size, and buying power
Require a fresh collector chain snapshot
Fetch spot, prior close, regular-session open, VIX, and current chain
GapRegimeFilter and DirectionFilter/BiasScoreFilter choose CALL or PUT
select_entry_candidate builds candidates across active widths and applies TARGET_COST, VIX, or BEST_RR selection
Persist candidates and selection-parity audit data
OrderManager fills paper entries immediately at observed mark plus commission; live mode walks the debit-price ladder and creates broker order intents
Persist butterfly_trades, update risk state/metrics, notify Discord
Pass TradeRecord and ButterflyCandidate to PositionService
position_and_exit: Poll current option chain every 2 seconds
PositionManager computes fly value, quote quality, tent boundaries, PnL, accepted peak, and time regime
Persist monitoring_leg_quotes, peak_value, tent_boundaries, and decision events
ProfitStateMachine evaluates configured peak trailer or profit protector policy
On signal, capture exit-mark parity and pending-exit metadata
OrderManager executes exit; failures leave the position OPEN for retry
On fill or cash settlement, close trade, record PnL in RiskEngine, update metrics, and notify
backtest_and_research: run_backtest_db loads TimescaleDB chain/spot history and the asset config, scans the configured entry window, and uses only VIX observations available at each candidate time
It shares strategy.entry_selection with live entry selection; hypothetical sweeps use collector snapshots while live-pinned replay may also use recorded monitoring legs
SimulationEngine and loaders support CSV, DB, Schwab, cache, synthetic, sweep, and legacy experiments
inspect_entry and parity reports explain historical decisions and live-vs-replay differences
run_entry_analysis and SimulationEngine.simulate_day are legacy research paths, not live-parity evidence
equity_morning_scan: Weekly refresh seeds NASDAQ/NYSE symbols and validates price and 20-day average volume through Schwab
Morning scan loads configured universes and batches Schwab equity quotes
Volume module adds RVOL and prior-session changes
News module adds SEC and optional Alpha Vantage catalysts
Scanner filters/ranks movers, opening focus, catalysts, and market context
Report module archives text/JSON and Discord sends the report
reporting: Daily report card combines Schwab balances, transactions, orders, positions, and candle charts
Live performance reads trade/decision data and renders HTML plus performance charts
Weekend review produces weekly/month-to-date summaries and end-of-day charts
Ladder and parity scripts interrogate persisted audit evidence

modules.responsibility: Configuration, logging, metrics, and market-time utilities
Schwab boundary, collection, chain normalization, and domain schemas
asyncpg pool, migrations, and repository-style query classes
Direction, regime, construction, width selection, candidate selection, and parity
Schwab butterfly order payloads, paper fills, live price ladder, status parsing, and intent persistence
Conservative pre-entry guards and persistent daily/weekly loss accounting
Butterfly valuation, quote-quality filters, peak tracking, tent calculations, and exit policy
Use-case orchestration, notifications, charts, calendars, report delivery, and weekend review
Historical/synthetic loading, caching, simulation models, and performance metrics
Black-Scholes pricing, IV skew model, and synthetic option chains
Universe maintenance, quote parsing, filters/ranking, volume enrichment, catalysts, and reports
Daily account report, live performance HTML, drawdowns, and PNG charts

tables.name: butterfly_candidates
tables.meaning: Candidates from each initial scan, with selected flag

tables.name: decision_log
tables.meaning: Audit trail for significant decisions and failures

tables.name: daily_risk_state
tables.meaning: Per-date, per-underlying trade count, PnL, and halt state

tables.name: tent_boundaries
tables.meaning: Calculated position tent limits over time

tables.name: broker_order_intents
tables.meaning: Durable live entry/exit order intent and broker evidence

entrypoints.risk: high; may place orders when live guards are enabled

entrypoints.purpose: Preferred config-backed historical replay and sweeps
Equity quote/news scan and Discord report
Compare live selection to replay selection
Reconstruct exit marks from recorded legs

asset_configs.role: BEST_RR paper-only forward test with separate DB and notifications disabled

configuration.secret_rule: Never print, summarize, commit, or rewrite secret values.

safety_invariants: SPX is primary; NDX and XSP do not imply production parity.
Paper trading is the default; live trading needs execution.allow_live_trading or ALLOW_LIVE_TRADING=true.
Do not alter account guards, order routing, loss/trade limits, paper/live mode, or tokens without direct user authorization.
Live startup/reconciliation must fail closed when broker evidence is ambiguous.
At most one active live entry intent per underlying/date and one active exit intent per trade are enforced in SQL.
At most one OPEN trade per underlying/date is enforced by migration 008.
Paper fill pricing must preserve the project mark-price convention.
The SPX candidate remains paper-only, uses a separate database, and cannot replace the primary before its 20-trade forward review.
TimescaleDB history plus decision/intents evidence is the audit and replay backbone.

known_caveats: The graph report lists 1972 isolated nodes and many inferred edges; confirm behavior in code/tests.
README labels SimulationEngine.simulate_day and run_entry_analysis as legacy for parity purposes.
CLAUDE.md has useful context but AGENTS.md takes precedence.
Do not run commands that can place orders or call Schwab write APIs without stating the risk first.
